name: um1_3d_volume
description: **Codebase Intelligence:** This JavaScript codebase contains 266 extracted functions across 111 files. Key extracted functionality includes: create_line_volume, create_point_volume, create_reconstructed_line_volume, detect_3d_cells_blob, load_tiff_volume, and more.

divide_tiff.py.name: divide_tiff_into_quadrants
divide_tiff.py.docstring:
Divided a 3D TIFF volume into 4 spatial quadrants (2x2 grid) 
memory-efficiently by processing slice-by-slice.

visualize_napari_csv.py.name: load_csv_points
visualize_napari_csv.py.docstring: Loads points from a CSV file (x,y,z,gene).

centroid_cw_processing\\utils.py.name: parse_slice_num
centroid_cw_processing\\utils.py.docstring:
Extracts the integer slice number from the filename.
Example: A3_ch04_slice0001.swc -> 1

centroid_cw_processing\\utils.py.name: parse_swc_files
centroid_cw_processing\\utils.py.docstring: Parses all SWC files in the input directory and returns a list of nodes.

centroid_cw_processing\\utils.py.name: serialize_to_cw_complex
centroid_cw_processing\\utils.py.docstring: Serializes the list of nodes to a CW Complex JSON file.

cloud_proofreading\\napari_stream\\unifi.name: parse_swc_skeleton
cloud_proofreading\\napari_stream\\unifi.docstring: Parses an SWC file into paths (lines) and extracts original attributes.

cloud_proofreading\\napari_stream\\unifi.name: save_swc_skeleton
cloud_proofreading\\napari_stream\\unifi.docstring:
Saves edited napari paths back to an SWC format.
Uses a KD-Tree to preserve original types and radiuses if nodes were slightly moved.

cloud_proofreading\\napari_stream\\unifi.name: color_connected_components
cloud_proofreading\\napari_stream\\unifi.docstring: Identifies connected paths (unique trees) and assigns a distinct color to each tree.

docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline.py.name: load_swc
docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline.py.docstring: Loads centroids from an SWC file into a numpy array (x, y, z).

docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline.py.name: save_swc
docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline.py.docstring: Saves centroids to an SWC file.

docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline.py.name: save_scaled_swc
docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline.py.docstring: Applies scale factors and saves to SWC.

docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline_para.name: process_block
docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline_para.docstring: Function to process a single block, designed for parallel execution.

docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline_para.name: detect_centroids_parallel
docker_cell_detection\\run_pipeline_para.docstring: Parallelized detection using a process pool.

docker_cell_detection\\visualize_3d.py.name: load_swc
docker_cell_detection\\visualize_3d.py.docstring: Loads centroids from an SWC file.

docker_cell_detection\\visualize_napari..name: load_swc
docker_cell_detection\\visualize_napari..docstring: Loads centroids from an SWC file.

neurite_detection\\pipeline\\core\\neuri.name: eigh_3x3_analytical
neurite_detection\\pipeline\\core\\neuri.docstring: Analytically solves the roots of a 3x3 symmetric matrix characteristic polynomial using Cardano's method

neuron_detection\\graph_utils.py.name: stitch_graphs
neuron_detection\\graph_utils.py.docstring:
Merges multiple subvolume graphs into a single graph.
Welds boundary nodes that are within `merge_dist`.

neuron_detection\\graph_utils.py.name: graph_to_cw_complex
neuron_detection\\graph_utils.py.docstring: Saves the graph into the CW-Complex JSON format.

neuron_detection\\graph_utils.py.name: graph_to_swc
neuron_detection\\graph_utils.py.docstring: Saves the graph to SWC format.

neuron_detection\\io_utils.py.name: load_tiff_memmap
neuron_detection\\io_utils.py.docstring: Safely loads a large TIFF file as a memory-mapped numpy array.

neuron_detection\\io_utils.py.name: extract_subvolume
neuron_detection\\io_utils.py.docstring: Extracts a specific bounding box from the memory-mapped volume.

neuron_detection\\processing.py.name: preprocess_volume
neuron_detection\\processing.py.docstring: Normalizes the volume intensity to [0, 1] range.

neuron_detection\\processing.py.name: apply_frangi_3d
neuron_detection\\processing.py.docstring: Applies a multi-scale 3D Frangi vesselness filter.

neuron_detection\\processing.py.name: extract_skeleton
neuron_detection\\processing.py.docstring: Thresholds the vesselness map and extracts a 1-pixel wide skeleton.

neuron_detection\\processing.py.name: process_subvolume_to_graph
neuron_detection\\processing.py.docstring: Runs the full pipeline on a subvolume and extracts the networkx graph.

neuron_detection\\visualization.py.name: launch_napari
neuron_detection\\visualization.py.docstring: Launches the Napari viewer with the detection layers.

neuron_detection\\visualize_swc_volume.p.name: load_swc_segments
neuron_detection\\visualize_swc_volume.p.docstring:
Parses an SWC file and returns a list of line segments (paths) 
connecting each node to its parent, formatted for Napari's Paths layer.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\clu.name: compute_invariants
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\clu.docstring: Groups individual moments into the 54 rotationally invariant shells F_nl.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: load_centroids_from_json
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring: Load cell centroids from JSON file.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: build_edge_lines
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring: Build edge line data from CW complex 1D cells for Shapes layer.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: create_proofread_widget
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring:
Create a Napari dock widget for proofreading.

Uses magicgui for widget creation if available, otherwise
falls back to a simple QWidget.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: _do_proofread_action
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring: Execute a proofreading action on the currently selected point.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: launch_napari_viewer
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring:
Launch the Napari viewer with all layers and proofreading.

Parameters
----------
volume_path : str or None
    Path to raw DAPI TIFF volume.
labels_path : str or None
    Path to cell labels TIFF.
centroids_path : str or None
    Path to centroids JSON.
cw_path : str or None
    Path to CW complex JSON.
output_dir : str
    Output directory for proofread exports.

proofreading\\napari_proofreader.py.name: read_swc
proofreading\\napari_proofreader.py.docstring:
Reads an SWC file and returns a pandas DataFrame.
Format: id type x y z r p

proofreading\\napari_proofreader.py.name: save_swc
proofreading\\napari_proofreader.py.docstring:
Saves centroids coordinates (in physical units or pixel units, depending on scale) back to SWC.
Here coords are in physical units (Z, Y, X) because Napari displays/edits them scaled, 
but let's write them matching the original SWC structure.
coords: numpy array of shape (N, 3) representing (z, y, x) in physical space.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\batch_.name: compute_invariants
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\batch_.docstring: Groups individual moments into the 54 rotationally invariant shells F_nl.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\build_.name: extract
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\build_.docstring: Extract the shape descriptors INSTANTANEOUSLY via a single matrix multiplication.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\build_.name: reconstruct
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\build_.docstring: Reconstruct the 3D volume INSTANTANEOUSLY via Matrix cross-multiplication.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_3d_basis_physical
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the full 3D Zernike polynomial Z_{nl}^m on physical coordinates.

Parameters
----------
n : int
    Degree (n >= 0).
l : int
    Order (0 <= l <= n, and n - l is even).
m : int
    Azimuthal index (-l <= m <= l).
x_phys, y_phys, z_phys : np.ndarray
    Physical Cartesian coordinates.
R_max : float
    Physical radius of the bounding sphere.
    
Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    Complex values of the Zernike polynomial at each point, normalized such that
    the volumetric integral of the squared magnitude over the sphere evaluates to 1.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: _q_coeff
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Calculate the coefficient Q_{nl
u} for the 3D Zernike radial polynomial.
    Uses standard python math since n, l, nu are small scalars.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_radial_gpu
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the 3D Zernike radial polynomial R_{nl}(r) on the GPU.
r is a cupy array.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_spherical_gpu
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the Spherical Harmonic Y_l^m(theta, phi) on the GPU.
theta and phi are cupy arrays.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_3d_basis_physical_gpu
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring: Evaluate the full 3D Zernike polynomial Z_{nl}^m on physical coordinates using GPU.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_3d_basis_precomputed_gpu
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the Zernike polynomial using highly-optimized precomputed spherical coordinates.
Optionally accepts a precomputed radial array R_cached to avoid redundant recalculation.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: _q_coeff
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Calculate the coefficient Q_{nl
u} for the 3D Zernike radial polynomial.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_radial
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the 3D Zernike radial polynomial R_{nl}(r).

Parameters
----------
n : int
    Degree of the polynomial (n >= 0).
l : int
    Order of the polynomial (0 <= l <= n, and n - l is even).
r : np.ndarray
    Radial distances (usually r <= 1.0).
    
Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    Evaluated radial polynomial values at r.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_spherical
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the Spherical Harmonic Y_l^m(theta, phi).

Parameters
----------
l : int
    Degree of the harmonic (l >= 0).
m : int
    Order of the harmonic (-l <= m <= l).
theta : np.ndarray
    Polar angle in radians [0, pi].
phi : np.ndarray
    Azimuthal angle in radians [0, 2*pi].
    
Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    Evaluated complex spherical harmonic values.
    
Notes
-----
In scipy's sph_harm signature, the order of angles is (m, n, theta, phi) 
where theta is the azimuthal angle [0, 2pi] and phi is the polar angle [0, pi].
We wrap this to use standard physics notation where theta is polar [0, pi]
and phi is azimuthal [0, 2pi].

repo_insight: The 'um1_3d_volume' repository offers a sophisticated, polyglot suite of tools for advanced 3D volumetric data processing, analysis, and interactive visualization, primarily targeted at complex biological imaging applications, such as neurobiology, indicated by functionalities for neuronal detection and tracing. Its holistic capability encompasses the entire pipeline from raw TIFF volume ingestion and division, through intricate 3D reconstruction of lines, points, and entire volumes, to precise feature detection of cells and centroids within these volumes. The repository is intended to be used by researchers or image analysis professionals to process large-scale 3D microscopy datasets, reconstruct biological structures, model them as complex graphs (e.g., neuronal trees), perform topological analysis on these graphs using specialized MATLAB tools (cw_graph_tools), and then interactively visualize and proofread the results using a Napari-based viewer. Furthermore, the inclusion of robust deployment scripts for both cloud and on-premise GPU-accelerated environments, alongside a Django web application, suggests an intention for scalable, collaborative data management and a web-accessible platform for initiating analyses or reviewing outcomes. Key architectural highlights include a powerful MATLAB core for computationally intensive 3D image processing, blob detection, and graph-theoretic algorithms like Minimum Spanning Tree for complex structure analysis; a Python layer leveraging Napari for interactive multi-dimensional data visualization and real-time streaming, and Django for a full-fledged web interface providing a collaborative front-end. The reliance on JSON for graph data interchange between MATLAB and potentially other components, coupled with shell scripts for bare-metal GPU server setup and cloud/on-premise deployment, underlines a design for high-performance, distributed processing and a deployable, production-ready system for managing and exploring extensive 3D biological datasets.
